commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex app-server auto-approval path for full-yolo approvals, wires the flag through main and side-thread approval handling, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +82. Total +111 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction for the review findings: PR head line 100 auto-approves any supported approval method, and line 2481 derives main-run autoApprove from appServer after promotion to untrusted. I did not run the app-server tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are narrow approval-routing repairs that an automated worker can attempt on the PR branch or a replacement branch.

target.title: fix(codex): honor yolo app-server approval policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Auto-accept Codex app-server command/file approval requests only when the effective runtime policy is full yolo: `approvalPolicy: "never"` and `sandbox: "danger-full-access"`.
- Keep OpenClaw's before-tool-call/native hook policy in the path before the auto-approval result.
- Use side-thread binding policy and sandbox values when deciding whether side-thread approvals may auto-approve.

## Verification

- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 fnm exec --using v24.15.0 -- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --no-file-parallelism`
- One transient full-suite run failed in an unrelated timeout test; the failed test passed alone, the full four-file proof passed on rerun, and autoreview then completed cleanly.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server yolo runs could still open OpenClaw plugin approval prompts even though the runtime policy already allows command/file approval without prompting.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at rebased PR head with the bundled Codex app-server approval bridge, main run-attempt path, and side-question path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the four app-server regression files covering approval bridge, config policy boundaries, main run attempts, and side-thread approvals.

Evidence after fix: 4 test files passed with 330 tests. Regression coverage asserts command and file approvals auto-accept in full yolo, non-yolo policies do not auto-approve, and side-thread approvals use the binding's effective policy/sandbox.

Observed result after fix: full-yolo Codex app-server approvals return `acceptForSession` without opening plugin approvals, while policy denials and non-yolo configurations still route through the normal approval path.

What was not tested: a full live Codex app-server turn against a real model provider.
target.author: earlvanze
